data.title: Fluor misses Q4 estimates
data.description:
• Fluor press release (NYSE:FLR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.48 misses by $0.29.
  • Revenue of $4.3B (+13.2% Y/Y) misses by $120M.
  • Ending backlog of $28.5 billion; 79% reimbursable
  • Strong operating cash flow for 2024 of $828 million; highest OCF generation since 2015
  • Q4 share repurchases of $125 million; $300 million planned for 2025
  • $1.6 billion gain recognized from deconsolidation of NuScale investment
  • In consideration of the timing of new awards and the pace of execution on the existing backlog, we are establishing an adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2025 of $575 to $675 million and adjusted EPS of $2.25 to $2.75 per share. Estimates for 2025 assume a tax rate of 30 to 35% percent. We will share our view on the markets over the next four years at our upcoming strategy update event on April 2nd.

data.title: Medtronic reports mixed Q3 earnings; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Medtronic press release (NYSE:MDT): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.39 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $8.3B (+2.5% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Medtronic reported Q3 worldwide revenue of $8.292 billion, an increase of 2.5% as reported and 4.1% on an organic basis.

  • GuidanceMedtronic today reiterated its revenue growth and EPS guidance for FY25.

    The company continues to expect FY25 organic revenue growth in the range of 4.75% to 5%. The organic revenue growth guidance excludes the impact of foreign currency and revenue reported as Other. Including Other revenue and the impact of foreign currency exchange, if recent foreign currency exchange rates hold, FY25 revenue growth would be in the range of 3.4% to 3.8%.

    The company continues to expect FY25 diluted non-GAAP EPS in the range of $5.44 to $5.50 (vs $5.45 consensus). This includes an estimated -5% impact from foreign currency exchange based on recent rates. The company's guidance represents FY25 diluted non-GAAP EPS growth in the range of 4.6% to 5.8%.

data.title: Phoenix Motor forms a new subsidiary aimed at international opportunities
data.description:
Phoenix Motor Inc. (NASDAQ:PEV) announced on Tuesday the formation of wholly owned subsidiary EdisonFuture International Co. The new Hong Kong-based entity will focus on international opportunities, leveraging the company's advanced EV technologies and brand strengths to expand into global markets.

As part of Phoenix Motor's (NASDAQ:PEV) strategy plan, EdisonFuture International will serve as the company's international arm, aiming to establish a strong presence in key regions including the Caribbean, South America, Europe, and Asia.

EdisonFuture International will initially focus on leveraging Asian supply chain networks to support its expansion into international markets. The company plans to work closely with regional partners to introduce Phoenix Motor's proven EV technologies to commercial fleets, logistics providers, and municipalities looking to transition to sustainable transportation solutions.

Looking ahead, Phoenix Motor's existing brands, "PhoenixEV" and "EdisonFuture," will primarily serve U.S. customers with medium and heavy-duty commercial EVs and light-duty EVs, respectively, and new subsidiary is expected to drive global expansion by utilizing international supply chain resources, financial networks, and market development strategies.

"The formation of EdisonFuture International marks a significant milestone for Phoenix Motor as we extend our footprint beyond the U.S. and tap into the rapidly growing global EV market," stated CEO Denton Peng. "By establishing operations in Hong Kong, a key gateway to international business, we will optimize supply chain efficiencies, strengthen financial partnerships, and accelerate market penetration in high-growth regions," he added/

As part of the new initiative, Phoenix Motor (PEV) will explore strategic alliances and collaborations with international stakeholders to drive innovation, enhance product offerings, and support the global adoption of zero-emission transportation.

Shares of Phoenix Motor (PEV) were up 2.8% in premarket trading.

data.title: Entergy Non-GAAP EPS of $0.66 beats by $0.03
data.description:
• Entergy press release (NYSE:ETR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.66 beats by $0.03.
  • Entergy initiated its 2025 adjusted earnings per share guidance range of $3.75 to $3.95 vs $3.92 consensus

data.title: Atkore divests Northwest Polymers
data.description:
Atkore (NYSE:ATKR) has announced the sale of Northwest Polymers, a post-industrial and post-commercial plastic recycler based in Molalla and Aurora, Oregon.

The sale does not impact Atkore’s continued operations for the high-density polyethylene product, which primarily serves telecommunications, utility and datacom markets.

“The sale of Northwest Polymers aligns with our disciplined approach to capital allocation and our commitment to drive value creation for our shareholders,” Atkore president and CEO Bill Waltz said.

ATKR --1.85% premarket to $70.85

Source: Press Release

data.title: Wynn Resorts is seen getting a boost from its UAE presence
data.description:
Jefferies upgraded Wynn Resorts (NASDAQ:WYNN) to a Buy rating after having it set at Hold. The firm sees the development of Al Marjan Island and exposure to stable VIP/premium consumers as Macau recovers as positive catalysts for the casino stock.

Analyst David Katz highlighted that recent progress on Al Marjan Island increased the firm's comfort with the timing of Q1 of 2027 and property EBITDA of $390 million to $570 million by 2030. Katz and his team estimate the UAE property could be worth $13 per share.

"Street confidence in Al Marjan Island is growing. In our view, the key issue surrounding Al Marjan Island is the opening and actualization of the property. However, given Wynn received a casino license for the property and construction is progressing on schedule, concerns are falling. The acquisition of Crown London to bolster the property's customer database provides additional support for our confidence in the project."

The firm also anticipates the recovery should continue in Macau, with Wynn (NASDAQ:WYNN) maintaining low double-digit market share and low single-digit revenue and EBITDA growth. Wynn's (NASDAQ:WYNN) exposure to the VIP and premium mass consumer was noted to have driven +23% adjusted EBITDAR growth in FY24. For the current quarter, preliminary results from Chinese New Year were reported to be positive at the tail end, driven by full hotel occupancy and solid VIP turnover.

Wynn Resorts (WYNN) is also expected to see improved results in Las Vegas.

Looking ahead, Katz thinks there will be a shift in investor sentiment to more mid-range valuation levels (11X EBITDA) vs. the recent 8X to 9X trading range, which implies further upside. Jefferies boosted its price target on Wynn Resorts (WYNN) to $118 from $105.

Wynn Resorts (WYNN) is up more than 12% since the casino giant topped consensus estimates with its Q4 earnings report earlier in the month. The company reported that overall operating revenue was flat year-over-year at $1.84 billion. That tally was ahead of the consensus expectation for $1.77 billion. Operating revenue increased for the Wynn Palace (+$38.6 million) and the Las Vegas properties (+$2.7 million), while falling for Wynn Macau (-$22.5 million) and Encore Boston Harbor (-$4.5 million).

data.title: Restaurant Brands buys partners' equity stakes in Burger King China for ~$158M cash
data.description:
Restaurant Brands International (NYSE:QSR) subsidiary has acquired from TFI Asia Holdings BV and Pangaea Two Acquisition Holdings XXIII all their equity interests in Burger King China for approximately $158M in an all-cash transaction.

As a result, Restaurant Brands now owns nearly 100% of the business and will engage advisors to assist the company to identify a new local partner to inject primary capital into the business and become the controlling shareholder.

This transaction aligns with the company’s long-term strategy of partnering with experienced local operators while maintaining a primarily franchised business.

"We are thankful for TFI's and Cartesian's partnership over the years and their role in expanding the brand in China," said Rafael Odorizzi, President of Asia Pacific for RBI. "This transaction marks the beginning of a new chapter for Burger King in China and reinforces our commitment to long-term growth in the region as we identify a new local operating partner. We are committed to offering our guests high quality food and exceptional experiences in welcoming restaurants across China."

QSR +0.66% premarket to $64.95

Source: Press Release

data.title: GeneDx Holdings reports Q4 results
data.description:
• GeneDx Holdings press release (NASDAQ:WGS): Q4 adjusted net income of $16.8 million

data.title: Tri Pointe Homes GAAP EPS of $1.37 beats by $0.08, revenue of $1.23B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Tri Pointe Homes press release (NYSE:TPH): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.37 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $1.23B (-0.8% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Home sales revenue for the quarter was $1.2 billion, a decrease of 2%
    • New home deliveries of 1,748 homes compared to 1,813 homes, a decrease of 4%
    • Average sales price of homes delivered of $699,000 compared to $685,000, an increase of 2%
  • Net new home orders of 940 compared to 1,078, a decrease of 13%
  • Active selling communities averaged 146.8 compared to 159.3, a decrease of 8%
  • Backlog units at quarter end of 1,517 homes compared to 2,320, a decrease of 35%
  • Announced a new stock repurchase program authorizing the repurchase of up to $250 million of common stock through December 31, 2025
  • For the first quarter of 2025, the Company anticipates delivering between 900 and 1,100 homes at an average sales price between $685,000 and $695,000. The Company expects its homebuilding gross margin percentage to be in the range of 22.0% to 23.0% for the first quarter of 2025 and anticipates its SG&A expense as a percentage of home sales revenue will be in the range of 15.0% to 16.0%. Lastly, the Company expects its effective tax rate for the first quarter of 2025 to be approximately 26.0%.
  • For the full year of 2025, the Company anticipates delivering between 5,500 and 6,100 homes at an average sales price between $660,000 and $670,000. The Company expects its homebuilding gross margin percentage to be in the range of 20.5% to 22.0% for the full year of 2025 and anticipates its SG&A expense as a percentage of home sales revenue will be in the range of 11.0% to 12.0%. Lastly, the Company expects its effective tax rate for the year to be approximately 26.0%.
